entities.name: VEGETATIVE GROWTH RATE
entities.description: A physiological trait hypothesized to be useful in the improvement of grain yield in cereal crops.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced by a crop.

entities.name: CEREAL CROPS
entities.description: A category of grasses grown for their edible grains.

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAM
entities.description: A systematic effort to improve the genetic makeup of a crop.

entities.name: RAPID METHOD
entities.description: A method to estimate growth rate using one or two harvest dates instead of periodic sampling.

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Pennisetum americanum, the primary crop studied for growth rate evaluation.

entities.name: WEEDY RELATIVE
entities.description: Pennisetum ssp. stenostachyum, a weedy relative of pearl millet.

entities.name: WILD RELATIVE
entities.description: Pennisetum ssp. monodii, a wild relative of pearl millet.

entities.name: PERIODIC SAMPLING
entities.description: The process of taking samples every 2 weeks to determine the actual growth rate.

entities.name: VEGETATIVE DRY WEIGHT
entities.description: The weight of the plant's vegetative parts after drying, used to calculate growth rate.

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: A specific developmental stage of the plant used as a harvest date.

entities.name: MATURITY
entities.description: The final developmental stage of the plant used as a harvest date.
